title: Wi-Fi Troubleshooting: Can't Connect to Corporate Network
text:
Q: What should I do if I can't connect to the corporate Wi-Fi network?
A: Step 1: Ensure Wi-Fi is enabled on your device. For Windows, click on the network icon in the taskbar and toggle Wi-Fi on. For Mac, go to the Apple menu > System Preferences > Network and check Wi-Fi is turned on. Step 2: Check if you are within range of the corporate Wi-Fi network. Step 3: Forget the network by going to your Wi-Fi settings, selecting the corporate network, and choosing 'Forget'. Step 4: Reconnect by selecting the corporate network again and entering the password. The password format is typically 'CorpWiFi2023'. Step 5: Restart your device to refresh network settings. Step 6: If you still cannot connect, try resetting your network settings. On Windows, go to Settings > Network & Internet > Status > Network reset. On Mac, go to System Preferences > Network, select Wi-Fi, and click the minus sign to remove it, then re-add it. Step 7: If issues persist, contact the IT Helpdesk for further assistance.
